rationale: Weekly-refresh promotion wave 3 — the W40K domain-end restbatch. Originally two books (W40K-0591..0592): the Astra Militarum omnibus Soldiers of the Imperium (Death World / Kasrkin / Witchbringer + four shorts) and the Emperor's Children Chaos novel Renegades: Lord of Excess (both 2025, both page-rated). Brief 167 (2026-06-28) EXTENDS this same restbatch from 2 to 8 books (restbatch-extension precedent, Session 136 — NOT a new batch 061; W40K-0593..0598 fall in batch 060's slot W40K-0591..0600) with the six OFOB (Brief 166) gap books, web-researched by Cowork and entered verbatim from the ADD tab of source/OFOB-gap-books.xlsx: Deathworlder (W40K-0593), The Rose in Darkness (0594), The Rose at War (0595), Lelith Hesperax: Queen of Knives (0596), Our Martyred Lady (0597, audio_drama), The Long and Hungry Road (0598, short_story). All six are unrated (no Goodreads value captured in this additive pass). Net-new reference entities added for resolution: characters wulf_khan / saint_veres / lelith_hesperax / katarinya_greyfax, locations lazulai / opal / chertes, plus character-aliases for Augusta Santorus (→ existing sister_superior_augusta), Major Wulf Khan, Inquisitor Greyfax, Greyfax. Restbatch slot now 8/10 filled.

overrides.synopsis: Three novels and four short stories follow the soldiers of the Astra Militarum across the most brutal fronts of the 41st Millennium. In Death World by Steve Lyons, Catachan Jungle Fighters are sent to Rogar III to eliminate an ork warboss, only to find the planet's own ecosystem evolving to kill them. In Kasrkin by Edoardo Albert, an elite Cadian commando team led by Captain Obeysekera braves a hostile desert world and T'au-held territory to rescue a downed Imperial general whose capture could break the war effort. In Witchbringer by Steven B. Fischer, former Cadian officer and newly sanctioned primaris psyker Glavia Aerand is deployed to the fog-shrouded death world of Visage, where warp-touched terrain and a haunting premonition force her to confront both the enemy and her own power. Four accompanying short stories deepen the front-line portraits of soldiery, sacrifice, and survival across these campaigns.

The Emperor's Children have long since shattered into warring splinters, pillaging the galaxy to feed appetites that can never be sated. Xantine, lord of the warband known as the Adored and a self-styled son of Fulgrim, craves something grander than endless raiding. When he discovers Serrine — a wealthy planet rotting under corrupt misrule and cut off from the Imperium by warp storms — he sees not a prize to be plundered but a canvas for perfection. He will reshape this world in his image. But Serrine has its own festering secrets, treachery waits within the Adored's own ranks, and the daemon that shares Xantine's flesh has appetites of its own. As Xantine's grand vision begins to devour itself, the cruel truth at the heart of every Chaos ambition is laid bare.

flags.reason: No named loyalist Imperial military faction opposes the warband in available sources; only generic Imperial authorities and a Genestealer Cult are mentioned on Serrine.

overrides.synopsis: On the dying world of Lazulai, all but lost to the tyranids, Major Wulf Khan of the Catachan 903rd 'Night Shrikes' is handed one last mission: lead a squad through the apocalypse to recover a piece of archeotech that could doom or deliver the entire Lazulai System.

rating.reason: OFOB-gap addition (Brief 167) — no Goodreads rating captured in this additive pass; left explicitly unrated.

overrides.synopsis: Sister Superior Augusta of the Order of the Bloody Rose is sent to the shrine world of Opal to recover the skull of Saint Veres during its holiest festival — only to uncover a genestealer cult beneath the surface and a hive fleet bearing down on the planet.

overrides.synopsis: Lelith Hesperax — peerless Succubus of the Cult of Strife and undisputed champion of Commorragh's arenas — takes to the road alongside the enigmatic Ynnari death cult.

flags.reason: Source synopsis names only Commorragh (the Succubus's home arena city) as a mentioned setting; the novel is a road-journey whose en-route worlds are not specified in available sources, so no primary location is asserted.

overrides.synopsis: A rare glimpse inside the tyranid hive mind as a hive fleet's vanguard descends on the doomed world of Chertes, whose defenders mount a resistance that is ultimately consumed.

flags.reason: Hive-mind-POV short story with no named individual characters in the source (Lexicanum) — zero character junctions by design, not an omission. protagonist_gender and length_tier facets are intentionally omitted: there is no individual viewpoint character, and no length bucket fits a short story.
